name: Cao Jiong (Yuanshou)
page_title: Cao Jiong (Yuanshou) 曹冏 (元首) [Wei] – Romance of the Three Kingdoms Encyclopedia – Kongming’s Archives

sections.short-biography: Wrote a lengthy petition warning Cao Shuang that the Cao needed more power.
sections.details:
Traditional Chinese 
	曹冏 (元首)


	 Pronunciation 
	Cao2 Jiong3 (Yuan2shou3)


	 Pinyin (Tones) 
	Cáo Jǐong (Yuánshǒu)


	 Wade-Giles 
	Ts‘ao Chiung (Yüan-shou)
sections.biography-source-undefined:
Cao Jiong was styled Yuanshou. He was a descendant of Cao Shuxing, who was an older brother of Cao Teng. Cao Jiong was two generations above his tentative relative, Cao Fang. When Cao Fang was emperor, he was very young, so Cao Shuang oversaw the central government. Cao Jiong wrote a lengthy petition detailing the rise and fall of the dynasties prior Wei, hoping Cao Shuang would take it into account. However, Cao Shuang declined his advice. Cao Jiong was soon made Grand Administrator of Hongnong. He would attribute the lengthy petition to Cao Zhi because Cao Zhi was more famous than him.

《魏氏春秋》曰：曹冏字元首，少帝族祖也。是時，天子幼稚，冏冀以此論感悟曹爽，爽不能納，為弘農太守。少帝，齊王芳也。

《魏氏春秋》曰：冏，中常侍兄叔興之後，少帝族祖也。是時天子幼稚，冏兾以此論感悟曹爽，爽不能納。

帝嘗閱《六代論》，問志曰：「是卿先王所作邪？」志對曰：「先王有手所作目錄，請歸尋按。」還奏曰：「按錄無此。」帝曰：「誰作？」志曰：「以臣所聞，是臣族父冏所作。以先王文高名著，欲令書傳於後，是以假託。」帝曰：「古來亦多有是。」顧謂公卿曰：「父子證明，足以為審。自今已後，可無復疑。」

full_text:
Cao Jiong (Yuanshou) 曹冏 (元首)
ARomance of the Three KingdomsEncyclopedia officer profile.
Cao Jiong (曹冏), styledYuanshou (元首). Wrote a lengthy petition warning Cao Shuang that the Cao needed more power. Cao Jiong was affiliated with the Wei Kingdom.
